title: ferray-core: no structured/record dtype in DType enum
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's PyArray_Descr supports structured dtypes — arrays where each element is a struct with named fields, potentially nested. This enables things like: dt = np.dtype([('x', 'f4'), ('y', 'f4'), ('z', 'f4')]); points = np.zeros(100, dtype=dt); points['x']. ferray has FerrayRecord as a derive macro, but DType has no variant for structured types, and DynArray cannot hold record-typed arrays. A DType::Struct(Vec<FieldDescriptor>) variant or similar would allow runtime-typed structured array operations and improve .npy interop for structured data.
